blockingIssues.description: By-value method receivers are not modeled as consuming boundaries, so non-`Copy` bindings can still be reused after `self` method calls.
Drop cleanup is emitted as plain Go `defer`, so bindings declared in nested blocks or loop bodies drop at function exit instead of lexical scope exit.

Guard suppression only handles a subset of consuming expression contexts, so `let x = take(r)`, `return take(r)`, and similar value-producing paths can leave caller cleanup armed after ownership moves.
The feature's generic enum constructor path still erased concrete type arguments instead of preserving instantiated enum types end-to-end.
Whole-binding ownership checks only covered field access; enum payload destructuring in patterns remained unguarded.
Generic enum constructor inference accepts conflicting payloads when the same type parameter appears multiple times, inferring from the first occurrence instead of rejecting mismatches.
Codegen preserves direct `T` payloads only, so nested generic payload instantiations can fall back to `any` instead of retaining concrete types.
Imported stdlib package calls are still treated as consuming `ExprPath` calls in guard suppression, so non-consuming `http::...` calls can still suppress Drop cleanup incorrectly.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when implementation-first work is acceptable and how `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` should be reported, especially around the current tests-first rule.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Five ownership-boundaries reviews observed mismatches between `.factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md`'s `Write tests first` guidance and reported procedure status: sessions `c992798b-ac65-415b-be61-4f97e37a6c4c`, `a86d1a79-5d6e-4014-bc8a-5c5d872a9c32`, `990b4c0f-767f-4981-a7c1-bea3f069729d`, and `5e0a2f04-e4ab-417d-8df9-d514e79e8557` edited production code before tests while still reporting `followedProcedure: true`, whereas session `419a7d9e-aca8-4610-8d18-a09488dc7593` reported `followedProcedure: false` for a similarly implementation-first ownership/codegen fix.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make the ownership-feature validation requirement explicit that compile-time ownership changes should exercise both a negative compile path and a positive runtime path, and require `followedProcedure` to be false when one half is intentionally skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `ownership-cleanup-control-flow-and-returns` found only positive runtime cleanup coverage even though `.factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md` expects ownership-sensitive work to include both a negative compile path and a positive runtime path, while the handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true`.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature metadata for `ownership-whole-binding-and-generic-interop-paths` mixes generic-enum scope with `fulfills: [VAL-OWN-010]` only.
rejectedObservations.reason: Mission-planning ambiguity rather than an AGENTS.md, skill, library, or services update; better handled when the orchestrator creates follow-up fixes.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the pre-existing generic-enum constructor gap in the compiler-worker skill.
rejectedObservations.reason: Stale and feature-specific: later ownership work already changed this area, so preserving a temporary gap in shared guidance would now be misleading.

rejectedObservations.observation: Change shared guidance because `.factory/runtime/non-consuming-call.go` was left behind.
rejectedObservations.reason: The existing compiler-worker skill already requires cleanup of disposable runtime artifacts, so this was a worker-specific deviation rather than a missing shared-state rule.
